Complete visual identity for a small-batch ceramics brand.

Complete visual identity for a small-batch ceramics brand. The studio needed something that reflected the handmade quality of their work without slipping into craft-fair cliché.

Deliverables included logo, wordmark, packaging system, tissue paper, card stock, and a set of brand guidelines. The palette was drawn directly from the studio's most popular glaze colors.

Magazine layout design for a quarterly arts publication.

Magazine layout design for a quarterly arts publication. Working across print and digital editions, each spread negotiates the relationship between image, caption, and essay text: finding the rhythm that makes a reader linger rather than flip.

The challenge was always the same: how do you give a photograph room to breathe without letting the page fall apart?

A collection of hand-drawn illustrations for a children's book.

A collection of hand-drawn illustrations for a children's book. The project demanded a visual language that could be simultaneously naive and precise, loose enough to feel childlike, controlled enough to carry a narrative across sixty pages.

Pencil, ink, and watercolor, scanned and cleaned in Illustrator. Every character has three expressions and at least two costume variations.

Animated brand identity for a creative studio.

Animated brand identity for a creative studio. The brief was to make something that felt handmade even in motion, not slick corporate animation, but something with the texture of craft behind it.

Built in After Effects and delivered as a living system: logo animations, transitional elements, lower thirds, and a set of looping backgrounds that the studio could deploy across presentations and social.

Animated brand identity for a creative studio. The brief was to make something that felt handmade even in motion, not slick corporate animation, but something with the texture of craft behind it.

Built in After Effects and delivered as a living system: logo animations, transitional elements, lower thirds, and a set of looping backgrounds that the studio could deploy across presentations and social.
A series of oil paintings exploring domestic objects under natural light.

A series of oil paintings exploring domestic objects under natural light. Each composition draws from the quiet intimacy of everyday items: ceramic vessels, glass, cloth. Arranged and rearranged until the light tells you where to look.

The work began as a study in translucency and evolved into an inquiry into the domestic still life as a site of attention. What does it mean to slow down and look?

Experimental typographic posters combining letterpress and digital.

Experimental typographic posters combining letterpress and digital. The letterpress blocks were carved and printed in-studio, then overprinted with halftone textures and hand-inked distortions using digital processes.

The tension between the two processes, the press's physical imprint and the screen's reproducible precision, became the subject of the work itself.
